commentary_text: The sportsbook consensus assigns Brazil a 56% win probability versus the model's 39%—a 17-percentage-point gap favoring the home side. The market also narrows the Japan upset case to 18%, while the model puts it at 30%. This sharpened confidence in Brazil could reflect recent form, lineup changes, or injury status that the model's last update did not capture. The polymarket prices align closely with the sportsbooks, suggesting real-money traders see similar signals. The divergence is real and substantial, but the dashboard surfaces it as a tension to monitor rather than a judgment on which source has better information.
